   111→async def send_batch(
   112→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   113→    template_name: str,
   114→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   115→    keywords: tuple,
   116→    interactive: bool = True,
   117→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
   118→    dry_run: bool = False,
   119→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
   120→) -> Dict[str, int]:
   121→    """
   122→    Score candidates and send batch messages.
   123→
   124→    Args:
   125→        candidates: List of candidates to message
   126→        template_name: Message template name
   127→        variables: Template variables
   128→        keywords: Search keywords (for scoring)
   129→        interactive: Confirm each message
   130→        skip_messaged: Skip already-messaged candidates
   131→        dry_run: Simulate without sending
   132→        browser: Browser manager
   133→
   134→    Returns:
   135→        Dict with send statistics
   136→    """
   137→    if not candidates:
   138→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates to message.[/yellow]")
   139→        return {'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'cancelled': 0}
   140→
   141→    # Sort by score (highest priority first)
   142→    sorted_candidates = sort_candidates_by_score(
   143→        candidates,
   144→        search_keywords=list(keywords),
   145→    )
   146→
   147→    # --- Optional LLM scoring ---
   148→    llm_results: Dict[str, Dict] = {}
   149→    llm_enabled = False
   150→    try:
   151→        config = get_config()
   152→        llm_enabled = config.get("scoring.llm.enabled", False)
   153→    except Exception:
   154→        pass
   155→
   156→    if llm_enabled:
   157→        try:
   158→            from src.utils.llm_scorer import (
   159→                check_ollama_available,
   160→                llm_score_candidates_batch,
   161→            )
   162→
   163→            endpoint = config.get("scoring.llm.endpoint", "http://127.0.0.1:11434")
   164→            model = config.get("scoring.llm.model", "qwen2.5:7b")
   165→            timeout = config.get("scoring.llm.timeout_seconds", 60)
   166→            jd_config = config.get("scoring.jd") or {}
   167→
   168→            if check_ollama_available(endpoint):
   169→                console.print("[cyan]LLM scoring enabled, evaluating candidates...[/cyan]")
   170→
   171→                # Build candidate info list
   172→                candidates_info = [
   173→                    _build_candidate_info_for_llm(c) for c in sorted_candidates
   174→                ]
   175→
   176→                results = llm_score_candidates_batch(
   177→                    candidates_info, jd_config, endpoint, model, timeout,
   178→                )
   179→
   180→                # Merge LLM scores
   181→                llm_weight = config.get("scoring.weights.llm_score", 5)
   182→                total_weights = sum(
   183→                    v for k, v in (config.get("scoring.weights") or {}).items()
   184→                )
   185→                if total_weights <= 0:
   186→                    total_weights = 100
   187→
   188→                for c, r in zip(sorted_candidates, results):
   189→                    bid = _get_boss_id(c)
   190→                    if r and r.get("score") is not None:
   191→                        llm_results[bid] = r
   192→                        # Blend LLM score into existing score
   193→                        current = getattr(c, '_score', 0) if not isinstance(c, dict) else c.get("_score", 0)
   194→                        llm_contribution = int(round(r["score"] / 100.0 * llm_weight / total_weights * 100))
   195→                        new_score = min(current + llm_contribution, 100)
   196→                        if isinstance(c, dict):
   197→                            c["_score"] = new_score
   198→                        else:
   199→                            c._score = new_score
   200→
   201→                # Re-sort after LLM score merge
   202→                sorted_candidates.sort(
   203→                    key=lambda x: (x.get("_score", 0) if isinstance(x, dict) else getattr(x, "_score", 0)),
   204→                    reverse=True,
   205→                )
   206→
   207→                console.print(f"[green]LLM scoring complete: {len(llm_results)}/{len(sorted_candidates)} evaluated[/green]")
   208→            else:
   209→                console.print("[yellow]Ollama not available, skipping LLM scoring[/yellow]")
   210→        except Exception as e:
   211→            logger.warning(f"LLM scoring integration error: {e}")
   212→            console.print(f"[yellow]LLM scoring skipped: {e}[/yellow]")
   213→
   214→    # Display scored candidates
   215→    has_llm = bool(llm_results)
   216→    table = Table(title=f"Candidates Ranked by Score ({len(sorted_candidates)} total)")
   217→    table.add_column("Score", style="bold", width=6)
   218→    table.add_column("Name")
   219→    table.add_column("Title")
   220→    table.add_column("Company")
   221→    if has_llm:
   222→        table.add_column("LLM", width=40)
   223→    table.add_column("Boss ID", style="dim")
   224→
   225→    for c in sorted_candidates[:20]:
   226→        score = getattr(c, '_score', 0)
   227→        bid = _get_boss_id(c)
   228→        row = [
   229→            str(score),
   230→            c.name,
   231→            c.title[:30] if c.title else "",
   232→            c.company[:20] if c.company else "",
   233→        ]
   234→        if has_llm:
   235→            llm_info = llm_results.get(bid)
   236→            if llm_info:
   237→                row.append(f"[{llm_info['score']}] {llm_info.get('explanation', '')[:30]}")
   238→            else:
   239→                row.append("-")
   240→        row.append(bid[:20])
   241→        table.add_row(*row)
   242→
   243→    console.print(table)
   244→    if len(sorted_candidates) > 20:
   245→        console.print(f"[dim]...and {len(sorted_candidates) - 20} more[/dim]")
   246→
   247→    # Send messages
   248→    stats = await send_message_batch(
   249→        candidates=sorted_candidates,
   250→        template_name=template_name,
   251→        variables=variables,
   252→        interactive=interactive,
   253→        skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   254→        dry_run=dry_run,
   255→        browser=browser,
   256→    )
   257→
   258→    return stats
   259→

   261→async def run_pipeline(
   262→    keywords: tuple,
   263→    template_name: str,
   264→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   265→    location: Optional[str] = None,
   266→    max_pages: int = 3,
   267→    batch_pages: int = 0,
   268→    experience: Optional[str] = None,
   269→    education: Optional[str] = None,
   270→    salary: Optional[str] = None,
   271→    interactive: bool = True,
   272→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
   273→    dry_run: bool = False,
   274→    resume: bool = False,
   275→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
   276→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   277→    """
   278→    Run the full pipeline: search → score → batch send.
   279→
   280→    Args:
   281→        keywords: Search keywords
   282→        template_name: Message template name
   283→        variables: Template variables
   284→        location: Location filter
   285→        max_pages: Pages per keyword
   286→        batch_pages: Send after every N pages (0 = send all at end)
   287→        experience: Experience filter
   288→        education: Education filter
   289→        salary: Salary filter
   290→        interactive: Confirm each message
   291→        skip_messaged: Skip already-messaged candidates
   292→        dry_run: Simulate without sending
   293→        resume: Resume from last checkpoint
   294→        browser: Browser manager
   295→
   296→    Returns:
   297→        Pipeline results summary
   298→    """
   299→    if browser is None:
   300→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   301→
   302→    reset_session()
   303→
   304→    # Handle resume
   305→    checkpoint = None
   306→    start_page = 1
   307→    sent_ids: List[str] = []
   308→    seen_ids: Set[str] = set()
   309→
   310→    if resume:
   311→        checkpoint = CheckpointManager.load_latest()
   312→        if checkpoint:
   313→            # Validate that checkpoint parameters match current run
   314→            cp_params = checkpoint.params
   315→            cp_keywords = tuple(cp_params.get('keywords', []))
   316→            cp_template = cp_params.get('template', '')
   317→            if cp_keywords != keywords or cp_template != template_name:
   318→                console.print(Panel(
   319→                    f"[yellow]Checkpoint mismatch:[/yellow]\n"
   320→                    f"  Checkpoint: keywords={cp_keywords}, template={cp_template}\n"
   321→                    f"  Current:    keywords={keywords}, template={template_name}\n"
   322→                    f"[bold]Starting fresh instead of resuming.[/bold]",
   323→                    title="Checkpoint Mismatch",
   324→                    border_style="red"
   325→                ))
   326→                checkpoint = None
   327→            else:
   328→                console.print(Panel(
   329→                    checkpoint.summary,
   330→                    title="Resuming Pipeline",
   331→                    border_style="yellow"
   332→                ))
   333→                start_page = checkpoint.completed_pages + 1
   334→                sent_ids = checkpoint.sent_ids
   335→                seen_ids = checkpoint.seen_ids
   336→        else:
   337→            console.print("[yellow]No checkpoint found, starting fresh.[/yellow]")
   338→
   339→    if checkpoint is None:
   340→        checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   341→
   342→    # Pipeline header
   343→    console.print(Panel(
   344→        f"[bold]Keywords:[/bold] {', '.join(keywords)}\n"
   345→        f"[bold]Template:[/bold] {template_name}\n"
   346→        f"[bold]Pages:[/bold] {max_pages}\n"
   347→        f"[bold]Location:[/bold] {location or 'Any'}\n"
   348→        f"[bold]Experience:[/bold] {experience or 'Any'}\n"
   349→        f"[bold]Education:[/bold] {education or 'Any'}\n"
   350→        f"[bold]Salary:[/bold] {salary or 'Any'}\n"
   351→        f"[bold]Dry Run:[/bold] {'Yes' if dry_run else 'No'}",
   352→        title="Boss直聘 Pipeline",
   353→        border_style="cyan"
   354→    ))
   355→
   356→    total_stats = {'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'cancelled': 0}
   357→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   358→
   359→    if batch_pages > 0:
   360→        # Batch mode: search N pages, then send, repeat
   361→        for page_start in range(start_page, max_pages + 1, batch_pages):
   362→            page_end = min(page_start + batch_pages - 1, max_pages)
   363→            pages_this_batch = page_end - page_start + 1
   364→
   365→            console.print(f"\n[cyan]--- Batch: pages {page_start}-{page_end} ---[/cyan]")
   366→
   367→            # Search
   368→            batch_candidates = await search_all_keywords(
   369→                keywords=keywords,
   370→                location=location,
   371→                max_pages=pages_this_batch,
   372→                experience=experience,
   373→                education=education,
   374→                salary=salary,
   375→                browser=browser,
   376→                start_page=page_start,
   377→                seen_ids=seen_ids,
   378→            )
   379→
   380→            if not batch_candidates:
   381→                console.print("[yellow]No new candidates in this batch.[/yellow]")
   382→                continue
   383→
   384→            # Track seen IDs
   385→            for c in batch_candidates:
   386→                seen_ids.add(c.boss_id)
   387→
   388→            all_candidates.extend(batch_candidates)
   389→
   390→            # Send batch
   391→            batch_stats = await send_batch(
   392→                candidates=batch_candidates,
   393→                template_name=template_name,
   394→                variables=variables,
   395→                keywords=keywords,
   396→                interactive=interactive,
   397→                skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   398→                dry_run=dry_run,
   399→                browser=browser,
   400→            )
   401→
   402→            # Update totals
   403→            for key in total_stats:
   404→                total_stats[key] += batch_stats.get(key, 0)
   405→
   406→            # Track processed candidate IDs for checkpoint
   407→            sent_id_set = set(sent_ids)
   408→            for c in batch_candidates:
   409→                if c.boss_id not in sent_id_set:
   410→                    sent_ids.append(c.boss_id)
   411→                    sent_id_set.add(c.boss_id)
   412→
   413→            # Save checkpoint
   414→            checkpoint.save(
   415→                keywords=keywords,
   416→                template=template_name,
   417→                variables=variables,
   418→                company=(),
   419→                location=location,
   420→                max_pages=max_pages,
   421→                batch_pages=batch_pages,
   422→                skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   423→                interactive=interactive,
   424→                completed_pages=page_end,
   425→                sent_ids=sent_ids,
   426→                seen_ids=list(seen_ids),
   427→                dry_run=dry_run,
   428→            )
   429→    else:
   430→        # Non-batch mode: search all pages, then send all
   431→        all_candidates = await search_all_keywords(
   432→            keywords=keywords,
   433→            location=location,
   434→            max_pages=max_pages,
   435→            experience=experience,
   436→            education=education,
   437→            salary=salary,
   438→            browser=browser,
   439→            start_page=start_page,
   440→            seen_ids=seen_ids,
   441→        )
   442→
   443→        if all_candidates:
   444→            for c in all_candidates:
   445→                seen_ids.add(c.boss_id)
   446→
   447→            total_stats = await send_batch(
   448→                candidates=all_candidates,
   449→                template_name=template_name,
   450→                variables=variables,
   451→                keywords=keywords,
   452→                interactive=interactive,
   453→                skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   454→                dry_run=dry_run,
   455→                browser=browser,
   456→            )
   457→
   458→    # Final report
   459→    console.print(Panel(
   460→        f"[bold]Candidates found:[/bold] {len(all_candidates)}\n"
   461→        f"[green]Messages sent:[/green] {total_stats['sent']}\n"
   462→        f"[red]Failed:[/red] {total_stats['failed']}\n"
   463→        f"[yellow]Skipped:[/yellow] {total_stats['skipped']}\n"
   464→        f"[dim]Cancelled:[/dim] {total_stats['cancelled']}",
   465→        title="Pipeline Complete",
   466→        border_style="green"
   467→    ))
   468→
   469→    # Clean up checkpoint on successful completion (non-resume)
   470→    if not resume and checkpoint:
   471→        checkpoint.delete()
   472→
   473→    return {
   474→        'candidates_found': len(all_candidates),
   475→        **total_stats,
   476→    }

   479→def generate_pipeline_report(
   480→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   481→    stats: Dict[str, int],
   482→    keywords: tuple,

   484→) -> str:
   485→    """
   486→    Generate an Excel report for the pipeline run.

   488→    Args:

   497→    from openpyxl import Workbook
   498→    from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill

   518→
   519→    # Results sheet
   520→    ws_results = wb.create_sheet("Candidates")

   531→    for c in candidates:

   533→        ws_results.append([

   539→            c.salary_expect or "",
   540→            c.work_years or "",
   541→            c.education_level or "",
   542→            score,
   543→        ])
   544→
   545→    wb.save(output_path)
   546→    logger.info(f"Pipeline report saved to: {output_path}")

   105→        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
   106→    )
   107→
   108→    def __repr__(self):
   109→        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"
   110→
   111→
   112→class DatabaseManager:
   113→    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""
   114→
   115→    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
   116→        if db_path is None:
   117→            config = get_config()
   118→            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/boss.db')
   119→
   120→        db_file = Path(db_path)
   121→        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   122→
   123→        self.db_path = db_path
   124→        self.engine = create_engine(
   125→            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
   126→            echo=False,
   127→            poolclass=StaticPool,
   128→            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
   129→        )
   130→        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
   131→
   132→        self.init_db()
   133→        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")
   134→
   135→    def init_db(self):
   136→        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
   137→        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
   138→        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")
   139→
   140→        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
   141→        run_migrations(self.engine)
   142→
   143→    @contextmanager
   144→    def session_scope(self):
   145→        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
   146→        session = self.Session()
   147→        try:
   148→            yield session
   149→            session.commit()
   150→        except Exception:
   151→            session.rollback()
   152→            raise
   153→        finally:
   154→            session.close()
   155→
   156→    def drop_all(self):
   157→        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
   158→        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
   159→        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")
   160→
   161→
   162→# Global database manager instance
   163→_db_manager = None
   164→
   165→
   166→def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
   167→    """Get global database manager instance."""
   168→    global _db_manager
   169→    if _db_manager is None:
   170→        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
   171→    return _db_manager
   172→
   173→
   174→def reset_db_manager() -> None:
   175→    """Reset global database manager (for testing)."""
   176→    global _db_manager
   177→    _db_manager = None
   178→

1→"""Lightweight database migration system."""
     2→
     3→from typing import Callable, List, Tuple
     4→
     5→from sqlalchemy import text, inspect
     6→from loguru import logger
     7→
     8→_migrations: List[Tuple[int, str, Callable]] = []
     9→
    10→
    11→def migration(version: int, description: str):
    12→    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    13→    def decorator(fn: Callable):
    14→        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
    15→        return fn
    16→    return decorator
    17→
    18→
    19→@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
    20→def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    21→    """Ensure core tables exist."""
    22→    pass
    23→
    24→
    25→@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
    26→def _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    27→    inspector = inspect(conn)
    28→    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    29→    if "retry_count" not in columns:
    30→        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))
    31→
    32→
    33→def _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    34→    conn.execute(text("""
    35→        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
    36→            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    37→            description TEXT NOT NULL,
    38→            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    39→        )
    40→    """))
    41→    conn.commit()
    42→
    43→
    44→def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    45→    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    46→    row = result.fetchone()
    47→    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0
    48→
    49→
    50→def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    51→    """Apply all pending migrations."""
    52→    with engine.connect() as conn:
    53→        _ensure_version_table(conn)
    54→        current = _current_version(conn)
    55→
    56→        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
    57→        if not pending:
    58→            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
    59→            return
    60→
    61→        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
    62→            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
    63→            fn(conn)
    64→            conn.execute(
    65→                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
    66→                {"v": version, "d": description},
    67→            )
    68→            conn.commit()
    69→
    70→        final = _current_version(conn)
    71→        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")
    72→

1→"""Configuration loader and manager."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→from pathlib import Path
     5→from typing import Any, Dict
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→
     9→from src.exceptions.errors import ConfigurationError
    10→
    11→
    12→class Config:
    13→    """Configuration manager for Boss Assistant."""
    14→
    15→    def __init__(self, config_dir: str = None):
    16→        if config_dir is None:
    17→            # Use project root (where this source file lives) to find config/
    18→            config_dir = str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "config")
    19→        self.config_dir = Path(config_dir)
    20→        self._settings: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    21→        self._selectors: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    22→        self._message_templates: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    23→
    24→        self._load_all()
    25→
    26→    def _load_json(self, filename: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    27→        filepath = self.config_dir / filename
    28→        try:
    29→            with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    30→                return json.load(f)
    31→        except FileNotFoundError:
    32→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Configuration file not found: {filepath}")
    33→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    34→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid JSON in {filepath}: {e}")
    35→
    36→    def _load_all(self):
    37→        logger.info("Loading configuration files...")
    38→        self._settings = self._load_json("settings.json")
    39→        self._selectors = self._load_json("selectors.json")
    40→        self._message_templates = self._load_json("message_templates.json")
    41→        logger.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    42→
    43→    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    44→        """Get setting by dot-notation key."""
    45→        keys = key.split('.')
    46→        value = self._settings
    47→
    48→        for k in keys:
    49→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    50→                value = value.get(k)
    51→                if value is None:
    52→                    return default
    53→            else:
    54→                return default
    55→
    56→        return value
    57→
    58→    def get_selector(self, selector_path: str) -> str:
    59→        """Get selector by dot-notation path."""
    60→        keys = selector_path.split('.')
    61→        value = self._selectors.get('selectors', {})
    62→
    63→        for k in keys:
    64→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    65→                value = value.get(k)
    66→                if value is None:
    67→                    raise ConfigurationError(f"Selector not found: {selector_path}")
    68→            else:
    69→                raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid selector path: {selector_path}")
    70→
    71→        return value
    72→
    73→    def get_template(self, template_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    74→        template = self._message_templates.get(template_name)
    75→        if template is None:
    76→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Message template not found: {template_name}")
    77→        return template
    78→
    79→    def list_templates(self) -> list:
    80→        return list(self._message_templates.keys())
    81→
    82→
    83→_config_instance = None
    84→
    85→
    86→def get_config() -> Config:
    87→    global _config_instance
    88→    if _config_instance is None:
    89→        _config_instance = Config()
    90→    return _config_instance
    91→
    92→
    93→def reset_config() -> None:
    94→    """Reset global config instance (for testing)."""
    95→    global _config_instance
    96→    _config_instance = None
    97→

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17→
    18→    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19→    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20→    """
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23→        self.config = config or get_config()
    24→        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    25→
    26→        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0
    27→        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    28→        self._error_timestamps: list = []
    29→        self._pruned: bool = False
    30→
    31→    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    32→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    33→        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    34→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    35→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    36→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    37→
    38→    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    39→        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    40→        error_count = len(self._error_timestamps)
    41→        combined = captcha_count + error_count * 0.5
    42→
    43→        if combined >= 3:
    44→            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    45→        elif combined >= 2:
    46→            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    47→        elif combined >= 1:
    48→            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    49→        else:
    50→            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    51→
    52→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    53→            logger.warning(
    54→                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    55→                f"(captchas={captcha_count}, errors={error_count} in last 30min)"
    56→            )
    57→
    58→    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    59→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
    60→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    61→        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    62→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    63→
    64→    @property
    65→    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    66→        return self._throttle_factor
    67→
    68→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    69→        action_map = {
    70→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
    71→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
    72→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
    73→        }
    74→
    75→        if action_type not in action_map:
    76→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
    77→
    78→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
    79→
    80→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
    81→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
    82→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 35)
    83→
    84→        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
    85→        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
    86→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    87→            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
    88→
    89→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
    90→
    91→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
    92→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
    93→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
    94→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
    95→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
    96→            ).scalar()
    97→            return count or 0
    98→
    99→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
   100→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   101→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   102→            if action_type:
   103→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   104→            last_time = query.scalar()
   105→            return last_time
   106→
   107→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   108→        if not self._pruned:
   109→            self.prune_old_entries()
   110→            self._pruned = True
   111→
   112→        self._refresh_throttle()
   113→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   114→
   115→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   116→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   117→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   118→
   119→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   120→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   121→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   122→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   123→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   124→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   125→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   126→
   127→                if oldest_in_window:
   128→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
   129→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   130→                else:
   131→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   132→
   133→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   134→                action_type=action_type,
   135→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   136→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   137→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   138→            )
   139→
   140→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   141→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   142→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   143→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   144→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   145→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   146→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   147→
   148→                if oldest_in_window:
   149→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
   150→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   151→                else:
   152→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   153→
   154→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   155→                action_type=action_type,
   156→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   157→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   158→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   159→            )
   160→
   161→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time()
   162→        if last_action:
   163→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   164→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   165→                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
   166→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   167→                    action_type='any',
   168→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   169→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   170→                )
   171→
   172→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   173→
   174→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   175→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   176→            action_log = ActionLog(
   177→                action_type=action_type,
   178→                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
   179→                details=details or {}
   180→            )
   181→            session.add(action_log)
   182→        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   183→
   184→    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
   185→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
   186→        try:
   187→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   188→                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
   189→                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
   190→                ).delete()
   191→                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
   192→                return count
   193→        except Exception as e:
   194→            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
   195→            return 0
   196→
   197→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   198→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   199→
   200→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   201→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   202→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   203→
   204→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   205→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   206→
   207→        return {
   208→            'action_type': action_type,
   209→            'hourly': {
   210→                'used': hourly_used,
   211→                'limit': per_hour,
   212→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   213→            },
   214→            'daily': {
   215→                'used': daily_used,
   216→                'limit': per_day,
   217→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   218→            }
   219→        }
   220→
   221→
   222→async def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str, max_retries: int = 60, max_wait_per_retry: int = 3600) -> None:
   223→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   224→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
   225→        try:
   226→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   227→            return
   228→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   229→            if attempt >= max_retries - 1:
   230→                raise
   231→            wait = min(e.wait_time + 1, max_wait_per_retry)
   232→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries})...")
   233→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   234→
   235→
   236→_rate_limiter = None
   237→
   238→
   239→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   240→    global _rate_limiter
   241→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   242→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   243→    return _rate_limiter
   244→
   245→
   246→def reset_rate_limiter() -> None:
   247→    """Reset global rate limiter instance (for testing)."""
   248→    global _rate_limiter
   249→    _rate_limiter = None
   250→

1→"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation."""
     2→
     3→import math
     4→import random
     5→import asyncio
     6→import time
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from src.utils.config import get_config
     9→
    10→
    11→_session_start: float = time.monotonic()
    12→_action_count: int = 0
    13→
    14→
    15→def reset_session():
    16→    global _session_start, _action_count
    17→    _session_start = time.monotonic()
    18→    _action_count = 0
    19→
    20→
    21→def _lognormal_delay(min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    22→    mid = (min_val + max_val) / 2
    23→    mu = math.log(mid)
    24→    sigma = 0.4
    25→    delay = random.lognormvariate(mu, sigma)
    26→    return max(min_val, min(delay, max_val * 1.5))
    27→
    28→
    29→def _fatigue_multiplier() -> float:
    30→    elapsed_minutes = (time.monotonic() - _session_start) / 60.0
    31→    if elapsed_minutes < 10:
    32→        return 1.0
    33→    factor = 1.0 + 0.006 * min(elapsed_minutes - 10, 100)
    34→    return min(factor, 1.6)
    35→
    36→
    37→def _maybe_distraction_pause() -> float:
    38→    global _action_count
    39→    _action_count += 1
    40→    if _action_count < 5:
    41→        return 0.0
    42→    if random.random() < 0.03:
    43→        pause = random.uniform(5.0, 30.0)
    44→        logger.debug(f"Simulating distraction pause: {pause:.1f}s")
    45→        return pause
    46→    return 0.0
    47→
    48→
    49→def _time_of_day_multiplier() -> float:
    50→    import datetime
    51→    hour = datetime.datetime.now().hour
    52→    if 23 <= hour or hour < 7:
    53→        return 1.3
    54→    elif (10 <= hour <= 12) or (14 <= hour <= 17):
    55→        return 1.0
    56→    else:
    57→        return 1.1
    58→
    59→
    60→def get_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action') -> float:
    61→    config = get_config()
    62→
    63→    delay_ranges = {
    64→        'page_load': (
    65→            config.get('delays.page_load_min', 2),
    66→            config.get('delays.page_load_max', 5)
    67→        ),
    68→        'action': (
    69→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    70→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    71→        ),
    72→        'typing': (
    73→            config.get('delays.typing_min', 0.05),
    74→            config.get('delays.typing_max', 0.15)
    75→        ),
    76→        'scroll': (
    77→            config.get('delays.scroll_min', 0.5),
    78→            config.get('delays.scroll_max', 1.5)
    79→        ),
    80→        'message': (
    81→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    82→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    83→        ),
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    min_delay, max_delay = delay_ranges.get(delay_type, (0.5, 2.0))
    87→    base_delay = _lognormal_delay(min_delay, max_delay)
    88→
    89→    if delay_type != 'typing':
    90→        base_delay *= _fatigue_multiplier()
    91→        base_delay *= _time_of_day_multiplier()
    92→
    93→    if delay_type in ('action', 'page_load', 'message'):
    94→        base_delay += _maybe_distraction_pause()
    95→
    96→    return base_delay
    97→
    98→
    99→async def async_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
   100→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
   101→    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   102→
   103→
   104→def get_retry_delay(attempt: int, base_delay: float = 1.0, max_delay: float = 60.0) -> float:
   105→    delay = min(base_delay * (2 ** attempt), max_delay)
   106→    jitter = delay * random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25)
   107→    return max(0, delay + jitter)
   108→

1→"""Retry utilities for navigation and network operations."""
     2→
     3→from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential, retry_if_exception_type
     4→from patchright.async_api import Page, Error as PlaywrightError
     5→from loguru import logger
     6→
     7→
     8→@retry(
     9→    stop=stop_after_attempt(3),

    12→    before_sleep=lambda retry_state: logger.warning(

1→"""Input validation utilities."""
     2→
     3→import re
     4→from typing import Dict, Any
     5→
     6→from src.exceptions.errors import ValidationError
     7→
     8→
     9→def validate_boss_id(boss_id: str) -> str:
    10→    """Validate Boss直聘 candidate ID format.
    11→
    12→    Boss直聘 uses encrypted IDs (encryptGeekId), stored as zp_{id}.
    13→
    14→    Args:
    15→        boss_id: Boss直聘 candidate ID to validate
    16→
    17→    Returns:
    18→        Validated and cleaned ID
    19→
    20→    Raises:
    21→        ValidationError: If ID format is invalid
    22→    """
    23→    if not boss_id:
    24→        raise ValidationError("Boss ID cannot be empty")
    25→
    26→    boss_id = str(boss_id).strip()
    27→
    28→    if not re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$', boss_id):
    29→        raise ValidationError(f"Invalid Boss ID format: {boss_id}")
    30→
    31→    return boss_id
    32→
    33→
    34→def validate_keywords(keywords: str) -> str:
    35→    """Validate search keywords."""
    36→    if not keywords or not keywords.strip():
    37→        raise ValidationError("Search keywords cannot be empty")
    38→
    39→    keywords = keywords.strip()
    40→
    41→    if len(keywords) < 2:
    42→        raise ValidationError("Search keywords must be at least 2 characters")
    43→
    44→    if len(keywords) > 200:
    45→        raise ValidationError("Search keywords cannot exceed 200 characters")
    46→
    47→    return keywords
    48→
    49→
    50→def validate_template_variables(template: Dict[str, Any], variables: Dict[str, str]) -> None:
    51→    """Validate that all required template variables are provided."""
    52→    required_vars = template.get('variables', [])
    53→    provided_vars = set(variables.keys())
    54→    required_set = set(required_vars)
    55→
    56→    missing_vars = required_set - provided_vars
    57→
    58→    if missing_vars:
    59→        raise ValidationError(
    60→            f"Missing required template variables: {', '.join(missing_vars)}"
    61→        )
    62→

1→"""Shared captcha detection utility.
     2→
     3→Detects captcha elements on page and pauses for manual resolution.
     4→Boss直聘 may use Geetest slider captcha or similar mechanisms.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import asyncio
     8→import sys
     9→
    10→from patchright.async_api import Page
    11→from loguru import logger
    12→from rich.console import Console
    13→
    14→console = Console()
    15→
    16→CAPTCHA_SELECTORS = [
    17→    '[class*="captcha"]',
    18→    '[class*="verify"]',
    19→    '[id*="captcha"]',
    20→    '[class*="slider"]',
    21→    '[class*="geetest"]',
    22→    'iframe[src*="captcha"]',
    23→    '[class*="verify-wrap"]',
    24→    '[class*="slide-verify"]',
    25→]
    26→
    27→
    28→async def _async_input() -> str:
    29→    """Read a line from stdin without blocking the event loop."""
    30→    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    31→    return await loop.run_in_executor(None, sys.stdin.readline)
    32→
    33→
    34→async def check_captcha(page: Page, report_to_rate_limiter: bool = False) -> None:
    35→    """Detect captcha elements on page and pause for manual resolution."""
    36→    for selector in CAPTCHA_SELECTORS:
    37→        try:
    38→            elem = await page.query_selector(selector)
    39→            if elem and await elem.is_visible():
    40→                if report_to_rate_limiter:
    41→                    from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
    42→                    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
    43→                    rate_limiter.report_captcha()
    44→
    45→                console.print("[bold red]Captcha detected! Please solve it in the browser window.[/bold red]")
    46→                console.print("[dim]Press Enter here after solving the captcha...[/dim]")
    47→                await _async_input()
    48→                await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    49→                return
    50→        except Exception:
    51→            continue
    52→

1→"""Pipeline checkpoint manager for resume capability."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→import os
     5→import tempfile
     6→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     7→from pathlib import Path
     8→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set
     9→
    10→from loguru import logger
    11→
    12→
    13→CHECKPOINT_DIR = Path("data/.checkpoints")
    14→
    15→
    16→class CheckpointManager:
    17→    """Manages pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume support."""
    18→
    19→    def __init__(self, checkpoint_id: Optional[str] = None):
    20→        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    21→
    22→        if checkpoint_id:
    23→            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"{checkpoint_id}.json"
    24→        else:
    25→            ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    26→            self.checkpoint_path = CHECKPOINT_DIR / f"pipeline_{ts}.json"
    27→
    28→        self._data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    29→
    30→    def save(
    31→        self,
    32→        keywords: tuple,
    33→        template: str,
    34→        variables: dict,
    35→        company: tuple,
    36→        location: Optional[str],
    37→        max_pages: int,
    38→        batch_pages: int,
    39→        skip_messaged: bool,
    40→        interactive: bool,
    41→        completed_pages: int,
    42→        sent_ids: List[str],
    43→        seen_ids: List[str],
    44→        total_results: Optional[dict] = None,
    45→        mode: str = "recommend",
    46→        dry_run: bool = False,
    47→    ) -> None:
    48→        self._data = {
    49→            "created_at": self._data.get("created_at", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),
    50→            "updated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    51→            "params": {
    52→                "keywords": list(keywords),
    53→                "template": template,
    54→                "variables": variables,
    55→                "company": list(company) if company else [],
    56→                "location": location,
    57→                "max_pages": max_pages,
    58→                "batch_pages": batch_pages,
    59→                "skip_messaged": skip_messaged,
    60→                "interactive": interactive,
    61→                "mode": mode,
    62→                "dry_run": dry_run,
    63→            },
    64→            "progress": {
    65→                "completed_pages": completed_pages,
    66→                "sent_ids": sent_ids,
    67→                "seen_ids": seen_ids,
    68→                "total_results": total_results or {},
    69→            },
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        try:
    73→            tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
    74→                dir=self.checkpoint_path.parent, suffix=".tmp"
    75→            )
    76→            try:
    77→                with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    78→                    json.dump(self._data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    79→                os.replace(tmp_path, self.checkpoint_path)
    80→            except BaseException:
    81→                try:
    82→                    os.unlink(tmp_path)
    83→                except OSError:
    84→                    pass
    85→                raise
    86→            logger.debug(f"Checkpoint saved: {self.checkpoint_path}")
    87→        except Exception as e:
    88→            logger.error(f"Failed to save checkpoint: {e}")
    89→            raise
    90→
    91→    @classmethod
    92→    def load_latest(cls) -> Optional["CheckpointManager"]:
    93→        CHECKPOINT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    94→        files = sorted(CHECKPOINT_DIR.glob("pipeline_*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
    95→
    96→        if not files:
    97→            return None
    98→
    99→        mgr = cls(checkpoint_id="__loading__")
   100→        mgr.checkpoint_path = files[0]
   101→
   102→        try:
   103→            with open(files[0], "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   104→                mgr._data = json.load(f)
   105→            logger.info(f"Loaded checkpoint: {files[0]}")
   106→            return mgr
   107→        except Exception as e:
   108→            logger.error(f"Failed to load checkpoint: {e}")
   109→            return None
   110→
   111→    @property
   112→    def params(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   113→        return self._data.get("params", {})
   114→
   115→    @property
   116→    def progress(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   117→        return self._data.get("progress", {})
   118→
   119→    @property
   120→    def completed_pages(self) -> int:
   121→        return self.progress.get("completed_pages", 0)
   122→
   123→    @property
   124→    def sent_ids(self) -> List[str]:
   125→        return list(self.progress.get("sent_ids", []))
   126→
   127→    @property
   128→    def seen_ids(self) -> Set[str]:
   129→        return set(self.progress.get("seen_ids", []))
   130→
   131→    @property
   132→    def total_results(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   133→        return self.progress.get("total_results", {})
   134→
   135→    @property
   136→    def summary(self) -> str:
   137→        p = self.params
   138→        pr = self.progress
   139→        created = self._data.get("created_at", "unknown")
   140→        updated = self._data.get("updated_at", "unknown")
   141→        mode = p.get("mode", "recommend")
   142→        return (
   143→            f"Keywords: {p.get('keywords', [])}\n"
   144→            f"Template: {p.get('template', '')}\n"
   145→            f"Mode: {mode}\n"
   146→            f"Pages completed: {pr.get('completed_pages', 0)} / {p.get('max_pages', '?')}\n"
   147→            f"Candidates seen: {len(pr.get('seen_ids', []))}\n"
   148→            f"Messages sent: {len(pr.get('sent_ids', []))}\n"
   149→            f"Created: {created}\n"
   150→            f"Last updated: {updated}"
   151→        )
   152→
   153→    def delete(self) -> None:
   154→        try:
   155→            if self.checkpoint_path.exists():
   156→                self.checkpoint_path.unlink()
   157→                logger.debug(f"Checkpoint deleted: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   158→        except Exception as e:
   159→            logger.error(f"Failed to delete checkpoint: {e}")
   160→

1→"""Candidate scoring for priority-based messaging.
     2→
     3→Two-layer architecture:
     4→1. Rule engine: seniority, company tier, education, experience, jieba skill matching,
     5→   keyword relevance, R&D bonus — weighted and normalized to 100.
     6→2. Optional LLM scoring via Ollama (integrated in pipeline).
     7→
     8→When no scoring config is present in settings.json, behavior is backward-compatible
     9→with the original simple scoring.
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import re
    13→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    14→
    15→from loguru import logger
    16→
    17→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18→# Try importing jieba; fall back to substring matching if unavailable
    19→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20→try:
    21→    import jieba  # type: ignore
    22→
    23→    _HAS_JIEBA = True
    24→except ImportError:
    25→    _HAS_JIEBA = False
    26→    logger.debug("jieba not installed, skill matching will use substring fallback")
    27→
    28→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→# Constants
    30→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→
    32→SENIORITY_SCORES: Dict[str, int] = {
    33→    "CTO": 50, "CEO": 40, "COO": 35, "VP": 40,
    34→    "首席": 45, "总裁": 40, "副总裁": 40,
    35→    "总监": 35, "技术总监": 40,
    36→    "技术负责人": 35, "负责人": 30, "Tech Lead": 35,
    37→    "架构师": 35, "专家": 30, "staff": 30, "Staff": 30,
    38→    "principal": 35, "Principal": 35,
    39→    "资深": 25, "高级": 20, "Senior": 20, "senior": 20,
    40→    "经理": 15, "主管": 15, "lead": 15, "Lead": 15,
    41→    "中级": 10,
    42→    "初级": 5, "Junior": 5, "junior": 5,
    43→    "助理": 3, "实习": 1,
    44→}
    45→
    46→TIER1_COMPANIES = {
    47→    "字节跳动", "ByteDance", "腾讯", "Tencent", "阿里巴巴", "阿里", "Alibaba",
    48→    "百度", "Baidu", "美团", "Meituan", "京东", "JD", "拼多多", "PDD",
    49→    "华为", "Huawei", "小米", "Xiaomi", "网易", "NetEase", "快手", "Kwai",
    50→    "蚂蚁集团", "蚂蚁金服", "滴滴", "DiDi",
    51→    "Google", "谷歌", "Microsoft", "微软", "Apple", "苹果",
    52→    "Amazon", "亚马逊", "Meta", "Facebook",
    53→    "Netflix", "Uber", "OpenAI", "Anthropic",
    54→}
    55→
    56→TIER2_COMPANIES = {
    57→    "携程", "Trip.com", "B站", "bilibili", "哔哩哔哩",
    58→    "知乎", "Zhihu", "小红书", "得物", "SHEIN",
    59→    "商汤", "旷视", "依图", "寒武纪", "地平线",
    60→    "理想汽车", "蔚来", "小鹏", "大疆", "DJI",
    61→    "米哈游", "miHoYo", "莉莉丝", "叠纸",
    62→    "360", "新浪", "微博", "搜狐", "爱奇艺",
    63→    "OPPO", "vivo", "一加", "联想", "Lenovo",
    64→    "SAP", "Oracle", "IBM", "Adobe", "Salesforce",
    65→    "Stripe", "Airbnb", "Spotify", "Twitter", "X",
    66→}
    67→
    68→EDUCATION_SCORES: Dict[str, int] = {
    69→    "博士": 100,
    70→    "硕士": 80,
    71→    "本科": 60,
    72→    "大专": 40,
    73→    "中专": 20,
    74→    "高中": 10,
    75→}
    76→
    77→# Default weights (used when no config present)
    78→_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS: Dict[str, float] = {
    79→    "seniority": 15,
    80→    "company_tier": 15,
    81→    "education": 10,
    82→    "experience": 15,
    83→    "skill_match": 25,
    84→    "keyword_relevance": 10,
    85→    "rd_bonus": 5,
    86→    "llm_score": 5,
    87→}
    88→
    89→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    90→# Config helpers (lazy-loaded, cached)
    91→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    92→_cached_jd_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    93→_cached_weights: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None
    94→
    95→
    96→def _get_jd_config() -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    97→    """Load JD config from settings.json, cached after first call."""
    98→    global _cached_jd_config
    99→    if _cached_jd_config is not None:
   100→        return _cached_jd_config
   101→    try:
   102→        from src.utils.config import get_config
   103→        cfg = get_config()
   104→        jd = cfg.get("scoring.jd")
   105→        if jd:
   106→            _cached_jd_config = jd
   107→            return _cached_jd_config
   108→    except Exception:
   109→        pass
   110→    return None
   111→
   112→
   113→def _get_weights() -> Dict[str, float]:
   114→    """Load scoring weights from settings.json, cached after first call."""
   115→    global _cached_weights
   116→    if _cached_weights is not None:
   117→        return _cached_weights
   118→    try:
   119→        from src.utils.config import get_config
   120→        cfg = get_config()
   121→        w = cfg.get("scoring.weights")
   122→        if w and isinstance(w, dict):
   123→            _cached_weights = w
   124→            return _cached_weights
   125→    except Exception:
   126→        pass
   127→    _cached_weights = _DEFAULT_WEIGHTS
   128→    return _cached_weights
   129→
   130→
   131→def reset_scoring_cache():
   132→    """Reset cached config (useful for testing)."""
   133→    global _cached_jd_config, _cached_weights
   134→    _cached_jd_config = None
   135→    _cached_weights = None
   136→
   137→
   138→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→# Individual scoring factors (all return 0.0 – 1.0)
   140→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   141→
   142→def _score_seniority(title: str) -> float:
   143→    """Score based on job title seniority. Returns 0.0-1.0."""
   144→    if not title:
   145→        return 0.0
   146→    title_lower = title.lower()
   147→    best = 0
   148→    for keyword, points in SENIORITY_SCORES.items():
   149→        if keyword.lower() in title_lower or keyword in title:
   150→            best = max(best, points)
   151→    # Max seniority score is 50 (CTO)
   152→    return min(best / 50.0, 1.0)
   153→
   154→
   155→def _score_company(company: str) -> float:
   156→    """Score based on company tier. Returns 0.0-1.0."""
   157→    if not company:
   158→        return 0.0
   159→    company_lower = company.lower()
   160→    for t1 in TIER1_COMPANIES:
   161→        t1_lower = t1.lower()
   162→        if len(t1_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   163→            if (t1_lower == company_lower
   164→                    or (len(t1_lower) >= 3 and t1_lower in company_lower)
   165→                    or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t1_lower)):
   166→                return 1.0
   167→    for t2 in TIER2_COMPANIES:
   168→        t2_lower = t2.lower()
   169→        if len(t2_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   170→            if (t2_lower == company_lower
   171→                    or (len(t2_lower) >= 3 and t2_lower in company_lower)
   172→                    or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t2_lower)):
   173→                return 0.67
   174→    return 0.0
   175→
   176→
   177→def _score_education(education_level: Optional[str], preferred: Optional[str] = None) -> float:
   178→    """Score based on education level. Returns 0.0-1.0."""
   179→    if not education_level:
   180→        return 0.0
   181→    # Find the candidate's education rank
   182→    candidate_score = 0
   183→    for edu, points in EDUCATION_SCORES.items():
   184→        if edu in education_level:
   185→            candidate_score = points
   186→            break
   187→    if candidate_score == 0:
   188→        return 0.0
   189→    # If preferred education specified, score relative to it
   190→    if preferred:
   191→        preferred_score = EDUCATION_SCORES.get(preferred, 60)
   192→        if candidate_score >= preferred_score:
   193→            return 1.0
   194→        else:
   195→            return candidate_score / preferred_score
   196→    # Without preference, just normalize
   197→    return candidate_score / 100.0
   198→
   199→
   200→def _parse_experience_years(work_years: Optional[str]) -> Optional[int]:
   201→    """Parse Chinese experience strings like '5年', '3-5年', '10年以上' → int."""
   202→    if not work_years:
   203→        return None
   204→    # Try "X-Y年" pattern → take the higher number
   205→    m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*[-–~]\s*(\d+)", work_years)
   206→    if m:
   207→        return int(m.group(2))
   208→    # Try single number "X年"
   209→    m = re.search(r"(\d+)", work_years)
   210→    if m:
   211→        return int(m.group(1))
   212→    return None
   213→
   214→
   215→def _score_experience(work_years: Optional[str], min_years: Optional[int] = None) -> float:
   216→    """Score based on experience years. Returns 0.0-1.0."""
   217→    years = _parse_experience_years(work_years)
   218→    if years is None:
   219→        return 0.0
   220→    if min_years is None or min_years <= 0:
   221→        # Without min requirement, just normalize (cap at 15 years)
   222→        return min(years / 15.0, 1.0)
   223→    if years >= min_years:
   224→        # Meet or exceed — bonus for exceeding, capped at 1.0
   225→        return min(1.0, 0.8 + 0.2 * min(years / (min_years * 2), 1.0))
   226→    else:
   227→        # Below minimum
   228→        return years / min_years
   229→
   230→
   231→def _build_candidate_text(
   232→    title: str = "",
   233→    company: str = "",
   234→    skills: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   235→    work_experience: Optional[List[Dict]] = None,
   236→    basic_info: Optional[Dict] = None,
   237→) -> str:
   238→    """Concatenate all available candidate text for skill matching."""
   239→    parts = []
   240→    if title:
   241→        parts.append(title)
   242→    if company:
   243→        parts.append(company)
   244→    if skills:
   245→        parts.append(" ".join(skills))
   246→    if work_experience:
   247→        for exp in work_experience:
   248→            if isinstance(exp, dict):
   249→                parts.append(exp.get("company", ""))
   250→                parts.append(exp.get("title", ""))
   251→                parts.append(exp.get("description", ""))
   252→    if basic_info and isinstance(basic_info, dict):
   253→        for v in basic_info.values():
   254→            if isinstance(v, str):
   255→                parts.append(v)
   256→    return " ".join(parts)
   257→
   258→
   259→def _tokenize(text: str) -> set:
   260→    """Tokenize text using jieba or fallback to whitespace/punctuation splitting."""
   261→    if not text:
   262→        return set()
   263→    if _HAS_JIEBA:
   264→        return set(w.lower() for w in jieba.cut(text) if len(w.strip()) > 0)
   265→    # Fallback: split on whitespace + punctuation, return lowered tokens
   266→    tokens = re.split(r"[\s,，;；、/|]+", text.lower())
   267→    return set(t for t in tokens if t)
   268→
   269→
   270→def _score_skills_jieba(
   271→    candidate_text: str,
   272→    candidate_skills: Optional[List[str]],
   273→    required_skills: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   274→    preferred_skills: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   275→) -> float:
   276→    """Score skill match using jieba tokenization. Returns 0.0-1.0."""
   277→    if not required_skills and not preferred_skills:
   278→        return 0.0
   279→
   280→    # Build candidate token set
   281→    tokens = _tokenize(candidate_text)
   282→    if candidate_skills:
   283→        for s in candidate_skills:
   284→            tokens.update(_tokenize(s))
   285→
   286→    def _match_skill(skill: str, token_set: set) -> bool:
   287→        skill_lower = skill.lower()
   288→        # Exact token match
   289→        if skill_lower in token_set:
   290→            return True
   291→        # Substring match in original text
   292→        if skill_lower in candidate_text.lower():
   293→            return True
   294→        # Tokenized skill match
   295→        skill_tokens = _tokenize(skill)
   296→        if skill_tokens and skill_tokens.issubset(token_set):
   297→            return True
   298→        return False
   299→
   300→    score = 0.0
   301→    total_weight = 0.0
   302→
   303→    if required_skills:
   304→        required_weight = 0.7
   305→        matched = sum(1 for s in required_skills if _match_skill(s, tokens))
   306→        score += required_weight * (matched / len(required_skills))
   307→        total_weight += required_weight
   308→
   309→    if preferred_skills:
   310→        preferred_weight = 0.3
   311→        matched = sum(1 for s in preferred_skills if _match_skill(s, tokens))
   312→        score += preferred_weight * (matched / len(preferred_skills))
   313→        total_weight += preferred_weight
   314→
   315→    if total_weight > 0:
   316→        return score / total_weight
   317→
   318→    return 0.0
   319→
   320→
   321→def _score_keyword_relevance(title: str, company: str, search_keywords: Optional[List[str]]) -> float:
   322→    """Score keyword relevance. Returns 0.0-1.0."""
   323→    if not search_keywords or not title:
   324→        return 0.0
   325→    title_lower = title.lower()
   326→    company_lower = (company or "").lower()
   327→    points = 0
   328→    for kw in search_keywords:
   329→        kw_lower = kw.lower()
   330→        if kw_lower in title_lower:
   331→            points += 10
   332→        elif kw_lower in company_lower:
   333→            points += 5
   334→    return min(points / 20.0, 1.0)
   335→
   336→
   337→def _score_rd_bonus(title: str) -> float:
   338→    """R&D role bonus. Returns 0.0 or 1.0."""
   339→    if not title:
   340→        return 0.0
   341→    rd_patterns = [
   342→        r"工程师", r"开发", r"架构", r"算法", r"研发",
   343→        r"engineer", r"developer", r"architect",
   344→    ]
   345→    for pattern in rd_patterns:
   346→        if re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE):
   347→            return 1.0
   348→    return 0.0
   349→
   350→
   351→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   352→# Main scoring function
   353→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   354→
   355→_UNSET = object()
   356→
   357→
   358→def score_candidate(
   359→    name: str,
   360→    title: str,
   361→    company: str,
   362→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   363→    *,
   364→    education_level: Optional[str] = None,
   365→    work_years: Optional[str] = None,
   366→    skills: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   367→    work_experience: Optional[List[Dict]] = None,
   368→    basic_info: Optional[Dict] = None,
   369→    jd_config: Any = _UNSET,
   370→    weights: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
   371→) -> int:
   372→    """Score a candidate with multi-factor weighted scoring.
   373→
   374→    Without keyword-only args, behavior is backward-compatible with the
   375→    original implementation: seniority + company tier + keyword + rd_bonus
   376→    scored on the original absolute scale.
   377→    """
   378→    # Determine if we should use enhanced scoring
   379→    has_enhanced_info = any([education_level, work_years, skills, work_experience, basic_info])
   380→
   381→    if jd_config is _UNSET:
   382→        # Only auto-load JD config when enhanced candidate data is available
   383→        jd = _get_jd_config() if has_enhanced_info else None
   384→    else:
   385→        jd = jd_config
   386→
   387→    if jd is None and not has_enhanced_info:
   388→        # Legacy mode: original absolute scoring
   389→        return _legacy_score(name, title, company, search_keywords)
   390→
   391→    # Enhanced mode: weighted factor scoring
   392→    w = weights if weights is not None else _get_weights()
   393→    total_weight = sum(v for k, v in w.items() if k != "llm_score")
   394→    if total_weight <= 0:
   395→        total_weight = 1.0
   396→
   397→    # Compute each factor (0.0-1.0)
   398→    seniority = _score_seniority(title)
   399→    company_tier = _score_company(company)
   400→    education = _score_education(
   401→        education_level,
   402→        jd.get("preferred_education") if jd else None,
   403→    )
   404→    experience = _score_experience(
   405→        work_years,
   406→        jd.get("min_experience_years") if jd else None,
   407→    )
   408→
   409→    # Skill matching
   410→    candidate_text = _build_candidate_text(title, company, skills, work_experience, basic_info)
   411→    skill_match = _score_skills_jieba(
   412→        candidate_text,
   413→        skills,
   414→        jd.get("required_skills") if jd else None,
   415→        jd.get("preferred_skills") if jd else None,
   416→    )
   417→
   418→    keyword_rel = _score_keyword_relevance(title, company, search_keywords)
   419→    rd_bonus = _score_rd_bonus(title)
   420→
   421→    # Weighted sum (exclude llm_score from rule engine)
   422→    factors = {
   423→        "seniority": seniority,
   424→        "company_tier": company_tier,
   425→        "education": education,
   426→        "experience": experience,
   427→        "skill_match": skill_match,
   428→        "keyword_relevance": keyword_rel,
   429→        "rd_bonus": rd_bonus,
   430→    }
   431→
   432→    weighted_sum = 0.0
   433→    for factor_name, factor_value in factors.items():
   434→        factor_weight = w.get(factor_name, 0)
   435→        weighted_sum += factor_value * factor_weight
   436→
   437→    # Normalize to 0-100 scale
   438→    score = int(round(weighted_sum / total_weight * 100))
   439→    return max(0, min(score, 100))
   440→
   441→
   442→def _legacy_score(
   443→    _name: str,
   444→    title: str,
   445→    company: str,
   446→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]],
   447→) -> int:
   448→    """Original scoring logic for backward compatibility."""
   449→    score = 0
   450→
   451→    title_lower = title.lower() if title else ""
   452→    best_seniority = 0
   453→    for keyword, points in SENIORITY_SCORES.items():
   454→        if keyword.lower() in title_lower or keyword in title:
   455→            best_seniority = max(best_seniority, points)
   456→    score += best_seniority
   457→
   458→    if company:
   459→        company_lower = company.lower()
   460→        company_matched = False
   461→        for t1 in TIER1_COMPANIES:
   462→            t1_lower = t1.lower()
   463→            if len(t1_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   464→                if (t1_lower == company_lower
   465→                        or (len(t1_lower) >= 3 and t1_lower in company_lower)
   466→                        or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t1_lower)):
   467→                    score += 30
   468→                    company_matched = True
   469→                    break
   470→        if not company_matched:
   471→            for t2 in TIER2_COMPANIES:
   472→                t2_lower = t2.lower()
   473→                if len(t2_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   474→                    if (t2_lower == company_lower
   475→                            or (len(t2_lower) >= 3 and t2_lower in company_lower)
   476→                            or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t2_lower)):
   477→                        score += 20
   478→                        break
   479→
   480→    if search_keywords and title:
   481→        keyword_score = 0
   482→        for kw in search_keywords:
   483→            kw_lower = kw.lower()
   484→            if kw_lower in title_lower:
   485→                keyword_score += 10
   486→            elif kw_lower in (company or "").lower():
   487→                keyword_score += 5
   488→        score += min(keyword_score, 20)
   489→
   490→    rd_bonus_patterns = [
   491→        r"工程师", r"开发", r"架构", r"算法", r"研发",
   492→        r"engineer", r"developer", r"architect",
   493→    ]
   494→    for pattern in rd_bonus_patterns:
   495→        if re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE):
   496→            score += 5
   497→            break
   498→
   499→    return score
   500→
   501→
   502→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   503→# Batch sorting
   504→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   505→
   506→def sort_candidates_by_score(
   507→    candidates: list,
   508→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   509→    *,
   510→    enrich_from_db: bool = True,
   511→) -> list:
   512→    """Sort candidates by score descending.
   513→
   514→    When enrich_from_db=True, batch-queries the DB for detailed profile data
   515→    (skills, work_experience, etc.) to enable deep scoring. Falls back to
   516→    basic scoring when DB data is unavailable.
   517→    """
   518→    # Batch load enrichment data from DB
   519→    enrichment: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
   520→    if enrich_from_db:
   521→        enrichment = _batch_load_enrichment(candidates)
   522→
   523→    scored = []
   524→    for c in candidates:
   525→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   526→            name = c.get("name", "")
   527→            title = c.get("title", "")
   528→            company = c.get("company", "")
   529→            boss_id = c.get("boss_id", "")
   530→        else:
   531→            name = getattr(c, "name", "")
   532→            title = getattr(c, "title", "")
   533→            company = getattr(c, "company", "")
   534→            boss_id = getattr(c, "boss_id", "")
   535→
   536→        # Get enrichment data if available
   537→        extra = enrichment.get(boss_id, {})
   538→
   539→        # Use enrichment data or fall back to candidate object attributes
   540→        education_level = extra.get("education_level") or (
   541→            c.get("education_level") if isinstance(c, dict) else getattr(c, "education_level", None)
   542→        )
   543→        work_years = extra.get("work_years") or (
   544→            c.get("work_years") if isinstance(c, dict) else getattr(c, "work_years", None)
   545→        )
   546→        skills_data = extra.get("skills") or (
   547→            c.get("skills") if isinstance(c, dict) else getattr(c, "skills", None)
   548→        )
   549→        work_exp = extra.get("work_experience") or (
   550→            c.get("work_experience") if isinstance(c, dict) else getattr(c, "work_experience", None)
   551→        )
   552→        basic_info = extra.get("basic_info") or (
   553→            c.get("basic_info") if isinstance(c, dict) else getattr(c, "basic_info", None)
   554→        )
   555→
   556→        s = score_candidate(
   557→            name, title, company, search_keywords,
   558→            education_level=education_level,
   559→            work_years=work_years,
   560→            skills=skills_data if isinstance(skills_data, list) else None,
   561→            work_experience=work_exp if isinstance(work_exp, list) else None,
   562→            basic_info=basic_info if isinstance(basic_info, dict) else None,
   563→        )
   564→
   565→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   566→            c["_score"] = s
   567→        else:
   568→            c._score = s
   569→
   570→        scored.append((s, c))
   571→
   572→    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
   573→
   574→    result = [item[1] for item in scored]
   575→
   576→    if result:
   577→        top = scored[0][0]
   578→        bottom = scored[-1][0]
   579→        logger.info(f"Candidates scored: top={top}, bottom={bottom}, count={len(result)}")
   580→
   581→    return result
   582→
   583→
   584→def _batch_load_enrichment(candidates: list) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
   585→    """Batch-load candidate details from DB."""
   586→    enrichment: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
   587→    boss_ids = []
   588→    for c in candidates:
   589→        bid = c.get("boss_id", "") if isinstance(c, dict) else getattr(c, "boss_id", "")
   590→        if bid:
   591→            boss_ids.append(bid)
   592→
   593→    if not boss_ids:
   594→        return enrichment
   595→
   596→    try:
   597→        from src.models.database import get_db_manager, Candidate
   598→        db = get_db_manager()
   599→        with db.session_scope() as session:
   600→            rows = session.query(Candidate).filter(
   601→                Candidate.boss_id.in_(boss_ids)
   602→            ).all()
   603→            for row in rows:
   604→                enrichment[row.boss_id] = {
   605→                    "education_level": row.education_level,
   606→                    "work_years": row.work_years,
   607→                    "skills": row.skills,
   608→                    "work_experience": row.work_experience,
   609→                    "basic_info": row.basic_info,
   610→                }
   611→        logger.debug(f"Enriched {len(enrichment)}/{len(boss_ids)} candidates from DB")
   612→    except Exception as e:
   613→        logger.debug(f"DB enrichment skipped: {e}")
   614→
   615→    return enrichment
   616→
